summary: Carole Herder reflects on talking to horses and how horses respond to the human voice, contrasting her quiet approach with her husband Greg's louder one.
Excerpt from Dr. Thomas Teskey's essay on how steel horseshoes restrict the hoof's natural blood-pumping mechanism and reduce circulation in the lower leg.
Carole Herder explains how to take a horse's digital pulse to detect hoof inflammation, abscess, bruising, or laminitis, and when to call a vet.
Carole Herder writes about the sense of smell in the horse-human relationship, describing how stress changed one horse's scent over time.
Carole Herder recaps her barefoot hoof care presentation at the 31st Annual Horse Expo at Michigan State University and the audience response.
Carole Herder argues against equestrian sport rules that ban barefoot and bitless horses, citing disqualifications at Blair Castle and Tevis Cup restrictions.
A customer testimonial on using Cavallo hoof boots with pastern wraps and studs to let Quarter Horses run safely in an indoor arena through winter.
Excerpt from Dr. Thomas Teskey's essay on resources for proper barefoot hoof trimming and Martha Olivo's United Horsemanship barefoot training organization.
A customer testimonial from a Peruvian mare owner at an ACTHA ride in Arizona, recommending Cavallo hoof boots and gel inserts on rocky terrain.
Excerpt from Dr. Thomas Teskey's essay invoking the Veterinarian's Oath to explain his unwavering conviction against shoeing horses with steel.
Carole Herder discusses proper hoof loading and the misconception that barefoot hooves wear away on hard roads, noting hooves adapt to their terrain.
A short note from Anne Riddell of Barefoot Horse Canada thanking Cavallo for sponsoring their well-attended seminars at the Can-Am show.
